risk_title: Political and Governance Instability Affecting Implementation
risk_summary: Political and governance fragility is rated as Substantial and persistent, creating systemic implementation challenges across the health program in Pakistan's federal and provincial structures.
evidence_quote: Political and Governance [Risk Category Rating]: Substantial [Previous Rating]: Substantial [Current Rating]: Substantial

risk_title: Institutional Capacity Constraints Linked to Governance Fragility
risk_summary: Institutional capacity for implementation is rated Substantial, with multiple delays in staffing and establishment of permanent PMU structures across provinces, indicating governance-related capacity gaps affecting project execution.
evidence_quote: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability [Risk Category Rating]: Substantial [Previous Rating]: Substantial [Current Rating]: Substantial... Not started yet. Expected to begin when the PMU staffing is complete.

risk_title: Staffing Delays Impacting Program Delivery in Fragile Context
risk_summary: Permanent PMU staffing delays are creating cascading implementation delays across multiple provinces, contingent on staffing completion for critical activities including governance, accountability, and service delivery initiatives.
evidence_quote: Contingent on permanent staffing in place, so expected by next fiscal year... It will be properly designed and established once permanent staff are in place.
